scenes.speaker: narrator
scenes.text: It was the day of the annual science fair at Delhi Public School. Little Arjun had been working on his project for two weeks.

scenes.speaker: arjun
scenes.text: Ma'am, ma'am! Look at my volcano! I made it with baking soda and vinegar! It really erupts, see!

scenes.speaker: teacher
scenes.text: Very good, Arjun! But remember, the judges want to know the science behind it. Can you explain the chemical reaction?

scenes.speaker: arjun
scenes.text: Umm... the baking soda and vinegar... they... umm... they don't like each other? So they fight and make bubbles?

scenes.speaker: narrator
scenes.text: The teacher tried very hard not to laugh. She knelt down beside Arjun and helped him practice.

scenes.speaker: teacher
scenes.text: Listen carefully. Baking soda is a base, and vinegar is an acid. When they mix, they create carbon dioxide gas. That's what makes the bubbles. Say it with me.

scenes.speaker: arjun
scenes.text: Baking soda is a base... vinegar is an acid... they make carbon dioxide gas... and that's the bubbles!

scenes.speaker: narrator
scenes.text: When the judges came, Arjun explained everything perfectly. His volcano erupted beautifully, and the judges were very impressed.

scenes.speaker: arjun
scenes.text: Ma'am! Ma'am! I got first prize! Thank you so much for helping me! You are the best teacher!

scenes.speaker: teacher
scenes.text: You did all the hard work, Arjun. I am very proud of you. Now go celebrate with your friends!
